issues.comment: Light-mode color regression — `.custom-message-length`. The original lightness was 70% but the first argument (light mode) in `dark-light-choose` was changed to 30%. The arguments appear to be swapped. The desktop version of the same selector correctly preserves 70% for light mode. This will make `.custom-message-length` text drastically darker in light mode on mobile.

Light-mode color regression — embedded-posts `.row a`. The original lightness was 30% but the first argument (light mode) in `dark-light-choose` was changed to 70%. The arguments appear to be swapped. This will make these links much lighter (washed out) in light mode.

Light-mode color regression — `.name`. The original lightness of 30% (relatively dark/prominent text) was changed to 50% in light mode. This silently makes the `.name` text lighter and less readable in light mode. Should be `dark-light-choose(scale-color($primary, $lightness: 30%), scale-color($secondary, $lightness: 70%))`.

Light-mode color regression — `.name`. Same issue as the desktop/user.scss bug but in the mobile stylesheet. The light-mode value was changed from 30% to 50%, making the `.name` text lighter and less readable in light mode.

Light-mode color regression — `h3`. The original lightness was 20% (near-black, prominent heading) but the first argument (light mode) in `dark-light-choose` was changed to 50%. The desktop equivalent correctly preserves `$lightness: 20%` in its `dark-light-choose`. This will make the `h3` heading significantly lighter/washed-out in light mode on mobile.
